import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.davinci.VectorLayerBuilder
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.Feature
import org.locationtech.jts.geom.{Coordinate, GeometryFactory}

val factory = new GeometryFactory()
val builder = new VectorLayerBuilder(100, "test")

builder.addFeature(Feature.create(
  factory.createPoint(new Coordinate(50, 50)), 
  Array("id", "name"),
  null, 
  Array[AnyRef](Int.box(10), "pt")
))

val layer = builder.build()
val featureCount = layer.getFeaturesCount

assert(featureCount == 1, s"Expected 1 feature in the built layer, but got $featureCount")
println(s"__CHECK__ build $featureCount")

import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path

// Use the parent directory of the provided raster_tif as the input directory
val rasterPath = new Path(raster_tif)
val dir = rasterPath.getParent.toString
val indexFile = new Path(output_dir, "_index.csv").toString

// Build the spatial index for the directory containing the GeoTIFF
edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor.RasterFileRDD.buildIndex(sc, dir, indexFile)

// Verify the index file was created and contains data
val fs = new Path(indexFile).getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
assert(fs.exists(new Path(indexFile)), "Index file was not created")

assert(fileLen > 0, "Index file is empty")

// Read the index file to verify its content

computeForFeaturesWithOutputSize.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.operations.GeometricSummary

val opts = new BeastOptions().set("iformat", "geojson")
val summary = GeometricSummary.computeForFeaturesWithOutputSize(featuresRDD, opts)

val expectedCount = featuresRDD.count()

assert(summary.numFeatures == expectedCount, s"Expected $expectedCount features, got ${summary.numFeatures}")
assert(summary.size > 0, s"Expected estimated size > 0, got ${summary.size}")

println(s"__CHECK__ computeForFeaturesWithOutputSize ${summary.numFeatures}, ${summary.size}")

config.code:
val desiredMBR = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.EnvelopeNDLite(2, 2.0, 3.0, 9.0, 8.0)
val numFeatures = 50

// Chain .config() calls to set generation parameters before calling the terminal .uniform() method
val randomPolygons = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.SpatialGeneratorBuilder(sc)
  .mbr(desiredMBR)
  .config(edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.UniformDistribution.MaxSize, "0.2,0.1")
  .config(edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.UniformDistribution.NumSegments, 10)
  .config(edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.UniformDistribution.GeometryType, "polygon")
  .config(edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.SpatialGenerator.Seed, 1794)
  .uniform(numFeatures)

// Force materialization and verify the generated data
val count = randomPolygons.count()
val firstGeom = randomPolygons.first().getGeometry.getGeometryType

assert(count == numFeatures, s"Expected $numFeatures features, but got $count")
assert(firstGeom.equalsIgnoreCase("Polygon"), s"Expected Polygon geometry, but got $firstGeom")

println(s"__CHECK__ config count=$count, firstGeom=$firstGeom")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.RasterMetadata

// Define a common grid to align rasters before an overlay operation
val targetMetadata = RasterMetadata.create(
  x1 = -124.0, y1 = 42.0, x2 = -114.0, y2 = 32.0, 
  srid = 4326, 
  rasterWidth = 1000, rasterHeight = 1000, 
  tileWidth = 100, tileHeight = 100
)

// Verify the metadata was created with the correct properties
assert(targetMetadata.srid == 4326, "SRID should match the input value of 4326")
assert(targetMetadata.rasterWidth == 1000, "Raster width should match the input value of 1000")
assert(targetMetadata.rasterHeight == 1000, "Raster height should match the input value of 1000")
assert(targetMetadata.x1 == -124.0, "x1 should match the top-left x-coordinate")
assert(targetMetadata.y1 == 42.0, "y1 should match the top-left y-coordinate")

println(s"__CHECK__ create ${targetMetadata.srid}, ${targetMetadata.rasterWidth}x${targetMetadata.rasterHeight}")

createDateFilter.code:
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor.HDF4Reader

// Create a filter for a specific date range using the required "yyyy.mm.dd" format
val dateFilter = HDF4Reader.createDateFilter("2001.02.15", "2005.02.11")

// Test paths against the filter
val pathInside = new Path("2003.07.15")
val pathBefore = new Path("2001.02.14")
val pathAfter = new Path("2005.02.12")

val isInsideAccepted = dateFilter.accept(pathInside)
val isBeforeAccepted = dateFilter.accept(pathBefore)
val isAfterAccepted = dateFilter.accept(pathAfter)

// Verify the filter correctly accepts and rejects paths based on the date range
assert(isInsideAccepted, "Path within the date range should be accepted")
assert(!isBeforeAccepted, "Path before the date range should be rejected")
assert(!isAfterAccepted, "Path after the date range should be rejected")

println(s"__CHECK__ createDateFilter accepted=$isInsideAccepted, rejectedBefore=$isBeforeAccepted, rejectedAfter=$isAfterAccepted")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.IndexHelper
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.IndexHelper.{Fixed, NumPartitions}
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.RSGrovePartitioner
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.IFeature

// Create a spatial partitioner using R*-Grove with disjoint boundaries
val partitioner = IndexHelper.createPartitioner(
  featuresRDD,
  classOf[RSGrovePartitioner],
  NumPartitions(Fixed, featuresRDD.getNumPartitions),
  (f: IFeature) => 1,
  "disjoint" -> true
)

// Apply the partitioner to physically distribute the RDD
val partitionedFeatures = featuresRDD.spatialPartition(partitioner)

// Force evaluation and verify correctness
val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()
val partitionedCount = partitionedFeatures.count()

assert(partitioner.isInstanceOf[RSGrovePartitioner], "Partitioner should be an instance of RSGrovePartitioner")
assert(partitioner.numPartitions > 0, "Partitioner should have created at least 1 partition")
as

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.synopses.Summary

val acc = Summary.createSummaryAccumulator(sc)

val mapped = featuresRDD.map { f =>
  acc.add(f)
  f
}

// Trigger a Spark action to evaluate the RDD and populate the accumulator
val rddCount = mapped.count()

// Retrieve the computed summary
val summary = acc.value

assert(summary.numFeatures == rddCount, s"Accumulator count ${summary.numFeatures} does not match RDD count $rddCount")

println(s"__CHECK__ createSummaryAccumulator ${summary.numFeatures}")

createTileIDFilter.code:
import java.awt.geom.Rectangle2D
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor.HDF4Reader

val rect = new Rectangle2D.Double(
  Math.toRadians(-145.0) * HDF4Reader.Scale,
  Math.toRadians(5.0) * HDF4Reader.Scale,
  Math.toRadians(29.0) * HDF4Reader.Scale,
  Math.toRadians(49.0) * HDF4Reader.Scale
)

val filter = HDF4Reader.createTileIDFilter(rect)

// h04v04 corresponds to approx lon [-140, -130] and lat [40, 50], which intersects the bounding box
val pathInside = new Path("MOD13Q1.A2021001.h04v04.006.2021017224531.hdf")
// h00v00 corresponds to approx lon [-180, -170] and lat [80, 90], which is outside the bounding box
val pathOutside = new Path("MOD13Q1.A2021001.h00v00.006.2021017224531.hdf")

val acceptedInside = filter.accept(pathInside)
val acceptedOutside = filter.accept(pathOutside)

assert(acceptedInside, "Filter should accept h04v04 which is within the bounding box")
assert(!acceptedOutside, "Filter should reject h00v00 which is outside the bounding box")

println(s"__CHECK__ createTileIDFilter acceptedInside=$acceptedInside, acceptedOutside=$acceptedOutside")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.EnvelopeNDLite
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.{SpatialGeneratorBuilder, UniformDistribution, SpatialGenerator}

val desiredMBR = new EnvelopeNDLite(0.0, 0.0, 100.0, 100.0)
val cardinality = 500L

val diagonalData = new SpatialGeneratorBuilder(sc)
  .mbr(desiredMBR)
  .config(UniformDistribution.GeometryType, "point")
  .config(SpatialGenerator.Seed, 12345)
  .diagonal(cardinality, percentage = 0.7, buffer = 2.0)

assert(count == cardinality, s"Expected $cardinality records, but got $count")

println(s"__CHECK__ diagonal $count")

val originalCount = df.count()
val originalCols = df.columns.toSet

// Encode the DataFrame for GeoParquet compatibility
val encodedDf = SpatialParquetSource.encodeGeoParquet(df)
val encodedCount = encodedDf.count()
val encodedCols = encodedDf.columns.toSet

// Verify that the count remains the same and the schema has been modified (geometry replaced by MBR and WKB)

assert(encodedCols != originalCols, "Schema should have changed after encoding (geometry column replaced)")
assert(encodedCols.size == originalCols.size + 1, "Schema should have exactly one more column (1 geometry replaced by 2 columns: MBR and WKB)")

filterPixels.code:
val origCount = rasterRDD.count()

// Filter keeping all pixels
val allRaster = RasterOperationsLocal.filterPixels(rasterRDD, (x: Float) => true)
val allCount = allRaster.count()

// Filter keeping no pixels
val noneRaster = RasterOperationsLocal.filterPixels(rasterRDD, (x: Float) => false)
val noneCount = noneRaster.count()

// Filter keeping pixels > 0.0f
val thresholdRaster = RasterOperationsLocal.filterPixels(rasterRDD, (x: Float) => x > 0.0f)
val thresholdCount = thresholdRaster.count()

// Verify the results
assert(allCount == origCount, s"Expected $origCount tiles when keeping all pixels, got $allCount")
assert(noneCount <= origCount, s"Expected <= $origCount tiles when keeping no pixels, got $noneCount")
assert(thresholdCount <= origCount, s"Expected <= $origCount tiles when filtering by threshold, got $thresholdCount")

println(s"__CHECK__ filterPixels all:$allCount none:$noneCount threshold:$thresholdCount")

findTransformationInfo.code:
val gf = new org.locationtech.jts.geom.GeometryFactory()
val point = gf.createPoint(new org.locationtech.jts.geom.Coordinate(-117.0, 34.0))

val t = Reprojector.findTransformationInfo(4326, 3857)
val convertedPoint = Reprojector.reprojectGeometry(point, t)

val cx = convertedPoint.getCoordinate.x
val cy = convertedPoint.getCoordinate.y

// EPSG:4326 (-117.0, 34.0) to EPSG:3857 should be roughly (-13024380, 4028802)
assert(cx < -13000000 && cx > -13100000, s"Unexpected X coordinate: $cx")
assert(cy > 4000000 && cy < 4100000, s"Unexpected Y coordinate: $cy")

println(s"__CHECK__ findTransformationInfo ${cx}, ${cy}")

flatten.code:
val flatPixels = rasterRDD.flatten

val pixelCount = flatPixels.count()
val tileCount = rasterRDD.count()

// Each tile must contain at least one pixel, so the total number of pixels 
// must be greater than or equal to the number of tiles.
assert(pixelCount >= tileCount, s"Pixel count ($pixelCount) must be >= tile count ($tileCount)")
assert(pixelCount > 0, "Flattened RDD should not be empty for a valid input raster")

val firstPixel = flatPixels.first()
val (x, y, metadata, value) = firstPixel

println(s"__CHECK__ flatten $pixelCount pixels, first pixel at ($x, $y) with value $value")

generate.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator._

val cardinality = 1000L
val randomBoxes = sc.generateSpatialData
  .distribution(GaussianDistribution)
  .config(UniformDistribution.GeometryType, "box")
  .config(UniformDistribution.MaxSize, "0.01,0.01")
  .generate(cardinality)

val count = randomBoxes.count()
assert(count == cardinality, s"Expected $cardinality generated features, but got $count")
println(s"__CHECK__ generate $count")

  UniformDistribution, 
  1000000L, 
  opts = Seq(
    "seed" -> 1, 
    UniformDistribution.MaxSize -> "0.1,0.1", 
    "geometry" -> "box"
  )
)

assert(count == 1000000L, s"Expected 1000000 geometries, but got $count")

println(s"__CHECK__ generateSpatialData $count")

geometryType.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._

// Compute the summary of the features RDD to get the spatial partition/summary object
val summary = featuresRDD.summary

// Call geometryType as a parameterless method
val geomType = summary.geometryType
val geomTypeStr = geomType.toString

// Verify that the returned geometry type is one of the documented inclusive types
val validTypes = Set(
  "Point", 
  "LineString", 
  "Polygon", 
  "MultiPoint", 
  "MultiLineString", 
  "MultiPolygon", 
  "GeometryCollection", 
  "Empty"
)

assert(
  validTypes.contains(geomTypeStr) || geomTypeStr.toLowerCase.contains("geometry"), 
  s"Unexpected geometry type returned: $geomTypeStr"
)

println(s"__CHECK__ geometryType $geomTypeStr")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

// Create a new BeastOptions instance and set some boolean-like string values
val opts = new BeastOptions()
opts.set("my-flag", "true")
opts.set("other-flag", "false")

// Retrieve the boolean values using getBoolean, providing defaults
val flag1 = opts.getBoolean("my-flag", defaultValue = false)
val flag2 = opts.getBoolean("other-flag", defaultValue = true)
val flag3 = opts.getBoolean("missing-flag", defaultValue = true)

// Verify that the parsed values match the expected boolean states and defaults
assert(flag1 == true, "my-flag should be parsed as true")
assert(flag2 == false, "other-flag should be parsed as false")
assert(flag3 == true, "missing-flag should fall back to the provided default of true")

getFeatureReaderClass.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.io.SpatialFileRDD

// Set up options with a format hint for GeoJSON
val opts = new BeastOptions().set(SpatialFileRDD.InputFormat, "geojson")

// Resolve the FeatureReader class for the given spatial file
val readerClass = SpatialFileRDD.getFeatureReaderClass(vector_geojson, opts)

// Verify the result
assert(readerClass != null, "Resolved FeatureReader class should not be null")
val className = readerClass.getName
assert(className.startsWith("edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast"), s"Expected a Beast FeatureReader implementation, but got: $className")

val totalFeatures = featuresRDD.count()

  .map(feature => feature.getGeometry)
  .filter(geom => geom != null)
  .count()

getLong.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

val options = new BeastOptions()

// Test 1: Missing key should return the default value
val defaultVal = options.getLong("non.existent.key", 9876543210L)
assert(defaultVal == 9876543210L, s"Expected default value 9876543210L, got $defaultVal")

// Test 2: Existing key should return the parsed Long value
options.set("existing.key", "1234567890123")
val existingVal = options.getLong("existing.key", 0L)
assert(existingVal == 1234567890123L, s"Expected existing value 1234567890123L, got $existingVal")

println(s"__CHECK__ getLong $existingVal")

getOperationParams.code:
val ops = edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.util.OperationHelper.operations.values
assert(ops.nonEmpty, "No operations registered in OperationHelper")

val opts = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions()
val opParams = ops.map { op =>
  val params = edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.util.OperationHelper.getOperationParams(op, opts)
  (op, params)
}

val (bestOp, maxParams) = opParams.maxBy(_._2.length)

assert(maxParams.length > 0, "Expected at least one operation to have parameters registered via @OperationParam")
assert(maxParams.forall(p => p.name != null && p.name.nonEmpty), "All extracted parameters must have a non-empty name")

println(s"__CHECK__ getOperationParams ${bestOp.getClass.getSimpleName} has ${maxParams.length} params")

val path = new org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path(raster_tif)
val fileSystem = path.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val reader = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.io.GeoTiffReader[Float]()
reader.initialize(fileSystem, raster_tif, "0", new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions())

val metadata = reader.metadata
// Calculate the center point of the raster in model (world) coordinates
val x = metadata.x1 + (metadata.x2 - metadata.x1) / 2.0
val y = metadata.y1 + (metadata.y2 - metadata.y1) / 2.0

// Ensure the point falls within a valid tile before querying
val tileId = metadata.getTileIDAtPoint(x, y)
val tile = reader.readTile(tileId)

// Extract the pixel value at the geographic coordinate
val value: Float = tile.getPointValue(x, y)

assert(false, "The documented contract is insufficient to verify the result of getPointValue without prior knowledge of the raster's expected pixel values.")
println(s"__CHECK__ getPointValue $value")

  in.close()

listFilesInZip.code:
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
import java.util.zip.{ZipOutputStream, ZipEntry, CRC32}
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.util.ZipUtil

// Create a temporary ZIP file in the output directory to test the API
val zipPath = new Path(output_dir, "test_archive.zip")
val fs = zipPath.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val outStream = fs.create(zipPath)
val zos = new ZipOutputStream(outStream)

val testFileName = "dummy.txt"
val testData = "Beast ZipUtil Test".getBytes("UTF-8")

// Use STORED method so we can read the uncompressed bytes directly via offset
val entry = new ZipEntry(testFileName)
entry.setMethod(ZipEntry.STORED)
entry.setSize(testData.length)
entry.setCompressedSize(testData.length)
val crc = new CRC32()
crc.update(testData)
entry.setCrc(crc.getValue())

zos.putNextEntry(entry)
zos.write(testData)
zos.closeEntry()
zos.close()

// 1. Call the API to list files in the ZIP
val contents: Array[(String, Long, Long)] = ZipUtil.listFilesInZip(fs, zipPath)

// 2. Verify the contents
assert(contents.length == 1, s"Expected 1 file in zip, found ${contents.length}")
val (fileName, byteOffset, byteSize) = contents.head

assert(fileName == testFileName, s"Expected filename $testFileName, got $fileName")
assert(byteSize == testData.length.toLong, s"Expected size ${testData.length}, got $byteSize")

// 3. Verify random-access read using the returned offset and size
val in = fs.open(zipPath)
in.seek(byteOffset)
val readData = new Array[Byte](byteSize.toInt) // Cast Long to Int for array size
in.readFully(readData)
in.close()

val readString = new String(readData, "UTF-8")
assert(readString == "Beast ZipUtil Test", s"Data read from offset does not match: $readString")

println(s"__CHECK__ listFilesInZip ${contents.length} files, first file: $fileName, size: $byteSize")

mbr.code:
val numRecords = 150
val minX = 5.0
val minY = 5.0
val maxX = 15.0
val maxY = 15.0
val desiredMBR = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.EnvelopeNDLite(2, minX, minY, maxX, maxY)

val generatedData = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator.SpatialGeneratorBuilder(sc)
  .mbr(desiredMBR)
  .uniform(numRecords)

val count = generatedData.count()
assert(count == numRecords, s"Expected $numRecords records, got $count")

val sample = generatedData.take(10)
val allWithin = sample.forall { f =>
  val env = f.getGeometry.getEnvelopeInternal
  env.getMinX >= minX && env.getMinY >= minY && env.getMaxX <= maxX && env.getMaxY <= maxY
}
assert(allWithin, "Generated features are outside the specified MBR")

println(s"__CHECK__ mbr $count")

  (_: edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.geolite.IFeature) => 1,
  new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions()
)

val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()
val partitionedCount = partitionedFeatures.count()

// RSGrovePartitioner is a disjoint partitioner, so the number of features should remain exactly the same
assert(originalCount == partitionedCount, s"Partitioned count $partitionedCount does not match original count $originalCount")
assert(partitionedFeatures.getNumPartitions > 0, "Expected the partitioned RDD to have at least 1 partition")

println(s"__CHECK__ partitionFeatures $partitionedCount")

val tile = rasterRDD.first()
val locationsSeq = tile.pixelLocations.toSeq

val validCount = locationsSeq.count { case (x, y) => tile.isDefined(x, y) }

assert(minY == 0, s"Expected local minY to be 0, got $minY")

assert(uniqueCount == count, "All pixel locations should be unique")

  opts

  }
  entry = zipStream.getNextEntry()
}

plotPyramid.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.RSGrovePartitioner
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path

val outZip = s"$output_dir/pyramid.zip"
val opts = new BeastOptions().set("mercator", true).set("stroke", "blue")

// Spatially partition the RDD before plotting to ensure distributed rendering
val partitioned = featuresRDD.spatialPartition(classOf[RSGrovePartitioner])

// Generate a 3-level image pyramid and write it to a zip archive
partitioned.plotPyramid(outZip, 3, opts = opts)

// Verify the output zip file was created and contains data
val outPath = new Path(outZip)
val fs = outPath.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
assert(fs.exists(outPath), "Output zip file was not created")

val fileSize = fs.getFileStatus(outPath).getLen
assert(fileSize > 0, "Output zip file is empty")

println(s"__CHECK__ plotPyramid $fileSize")

pointSample.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.synopses.PointSampler

val sampleSize = 1000
val sampleRatio = 0.5

// Call the static method on PointSampler with the pre-loaded featuresRDD
val sample: Array[Array[Double]] = PointSampler.pointSample(featuresRDD, sampleSize, sampleRatio)

// Verify the dimension-major structure
assert(sample != null, "Sample array should not be null")
assert(sample.length >= 2, s"Expected at least 2 dimensions (X and Y), but got ${sample.length}")

val numPointsX = sample(0).length
val numPointsY = sample(1).length

// Assert correctness of the returned structure and constraints
assert(numPointsX == numPointsY, s"Mismatch in dimension lengths: X has $numPointsX, Y has $numPointsY")
assert(numPointsX <= sampleSize, s"Number of sampled points ($numPointsX) exceeded the sampleSize limit ($sampleSize)")

println(s"__CHECK__ pointSample $numPointsX")

import java.io.{ByteArrayOutputStream, PrintStream}
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.util.OperationHelper

val baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
val printer = new PrintStream(baos)

// Retrieve the first available operation from the registry
val op = OperationHelper.operations.head._2

// Print its usage to the ByteArrayOutputStream
OperationHelper.printOperationUsage(op, null, printer)

printer.flush()
printer.close()

val usageString = new String(baos.toByteArray)

// Verify that the usage string was successfully generated and written
assert(usageString.nonEmpty, "Usage string should not be empty")
assert(usageString.length > 10, "Usage string should contain operation details")

println(s"__CHECK__ printOperationUsage ${usageString.length}")

rangeQuery.code:
import org.locationtech.jts.geom.{Envelope, GeometryFactory, PrecisionModel}

// Ensure we have data to query
val totalCount = featuresRDD.count()
assert(totalCount > 0, "Input featuresRDD must not be empty")

// Extract SRID to avoid CRS mismatch issues during the query
val firstGeom = featuresRDD.first().getGeometry
val srid = firstGeom.getSRID
val geomFactory = new GeometryFactory(new PrecisionModel(), srid)

// Get the bounding box of the entire dataset
val summary = featuresRDD.summary
val minX = summary.getMinCoord(0)
val minY = summary.getMinCoord(1)
val maxX = summary.getMaxCoord(0)
val maxY = summary.getMaxCoord(1)

// Query 1: Full bounding box (should return all features, potentially with duplicates if non-disjoint partitioned)
val fullRange = geomFactory.toGeometry(new Envelope(minX, maxX, minY, maxY))
val fullFilteredRDD = featuresRDD.rangeQuery(fullRange)
val fullCount = fullFilteredRDD.count()

assert(fullCount >= totalCount, s"Expected at least $totalCount features for full bounding box query, but got $fullCount")

// Query 2: Out-of-bounds bounding box (should return exactly 0 features)
val width = if (maxX > minX) maxX - minX else 1.0
val outOfBoundsRange = geomFactory.toGeometry(new Envelope(minX - width - 10, minX - 10, minY, maxY))
val emptyFilteredRDD = featuresRDD.rangeQuery(outOfBoundsRange)
val emptyCount = emptyFilteredRDD.count()

assert(emptyCount == 0, s"Expected 0 features for out-of-bounds query, but got $emptyCount")

println(s"__CHECK__ rangeQuery $fullCount")

val expectedCount = sc.shapefile(vector_shapefile).count()

val opts = new edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions()

  vector_shapefile, 
  "shapefile", 
  opts, 
  sc.hadoopConfiguration
)

val localFeatures = featuresIter.toList
val localCount = localFeatures.size

assert(localCount == expectedCount, s"Local read count $localCount does not match distributed read count $expectedCount")
assert(localCount > 0, "Expected non-empty shapefile to verify readLocal")

println(s"__CHECK__ readLocal $localCount")

val wktFile = new File(output_dir, "test_wkt.csv")

pw.println("id,name,geometry")
pw.println("1,point1,POINT (30 10)")

val wktPath = wktFile.getAbsolutePath

// Read the WKT file using the SparkContext extension method

val count = wktRdd.count()

assert(firstGeomType == "Point", s"Expected Point, got $firstGeomType")

println(s"__CHECK__ readWKTFile count=$count, firstGeomType=$firstGeomType")

reprojectEnvelopeInPlace.code:
val envelope = Array(-180.0, 0.0, 0.0, 90.0)

// Reproject the envelope in-place from WGS84 (4326) to Web Mercator (3857)
Reprojector.reprojectEnvelopeInPlace(envelope, 4326, 3857)

val x1 = envelope(0)
val y1 = envelope(1)
val x2 = envelope(2)
val y2 = envelope(3)

// Verify the coordinates were transformed to Web Mercator bounds
assert(x1 < -20000000.0, s"Expected x1 to be near -20037508.0, got $x1")
assert(math.abs(y1) < 1e-4, s"Expected y1 to be near 0.0, got $y1")
assert(math.abs(x2) < 1e-4, s"Expected x2 to be near 0.0, got $x2")
assert(y2 > 20000000.0, s"Expected y2 to be near 20037508.0, got $y2")

println(s"__CHECK__ reprojectEnvelopeInPlace [${envelope.mkString(", ")}]")

reprojectRDD.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.cg.Reprojector

val targetSrid = 3857
val projectedPolygons = Reprojector.reprojectRDD(featuresRDD, targetSrid)

val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()
val projectedCount = projectedPolygons.count()

assert(projectedCount == originalCount, s"Expected count $originalCount, but got $projectedCount")
assert(projectedCount > 0, "The featuresRDD was empty, cannot verify reprojection")

val firstGeom = projectedPolygons.first().getGeometry
val actualSrid = firstGeom.getSRID

// Beast's Reprojector sets the SRID on the resulting JTS geometries
assert(actualSrid == targetSrid, s"Expected SRID $targetSrid, but got $actualSrid")

println(s"__CHECK__ reprojectRDD $projectedCount, first SRID: $actualSrid")

saveAsIndex.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.RSGrovePartitioner
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path

val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()

// Spatially partition the RDD first to ensure disjoint partitions
val partitionedFeatures = featuresRDD.spatialPartition(classOf[RSGrovePartitioner])

// Save the partitioned RDD as an index
partitionedFeatures.saveAsIndex(output_dir, "rtree")

// Verify the output directory and _master file
val outPath = new Path(output_dir)
val fs = outPath.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val masterPath = new Path(outPath, "_master")

assert(fs.exists(masterPath), "Master file was not created by saveAsIndex")
val masterLen = fs.getFileStatus(masterPath).getLen
assert(masterLen > 0, "Master file is empty")

// Read back the indexed dataset to verify content
val readBackCount = sc.spatialFile(output_dir).count()
assert(readBackCount == originalCount, s"Expected $originalCount features, but got $readBackCount")

println(s"__CHECK__ saveAsIndex $readBackCount")

saveAsKML.code:
val outPath = s"$output_dir/test_output.kml"

// Save the vector features to KML format
// Coalesce to 1 partition to avoid distributed-write merge issues
featuresRDD.coalesce(1).saveAsKML(outPath)

// Read the output back as plain text to verify KML structure and content
val kmlLines = sc.textFile(outPath)
val hasKmlTag = kmlLines.filter(_.contains("<kml")).count() > 0
val placemarkCount = kmlLines.filter(_.contains("<Placemark>")).count()
val expectedCount = featuresRDD.count()

assert(hasKmlTag, "Output does not contain <kml> tag, invalid KML format")
assert(placemarkCount == expectedCount, s"Expected $expectedCount Placemarks, found $placemarkCount")

println(s"__CHECK__ saveAsKML $placemarkCount")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.RSGrovePartitioner
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.IndexHelper
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

// Spatially partition the RDD before saving, as required by saveIndex2
val partitioned = featuresRDD.spatialPartition(classOf[RSGrovePartitioner])

// Save the partitioned RDD to disk with a master index file
val opts = new BeastOptions().set("oformat", "geojson")
IndexHelper.saveIndex2(partitioned, output_dir, opts)

// Read the saved index back to verify correctness
val readBack = sc.spatialRDD(output_dir)
val count = readBack.count()
val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()

assert(count == originalCount, s"Expected $originalCount features in the saved index, but got $count")
println(s"__CHECK__ saveIndex2 $count")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.davinci.MVTDataVisualizer
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path

// Define visualization options
val opts = new BeastOptions().set("threshold", 0)

// Generate the multilevel visualization tiles from vector features
val tiles = MVTDataVisualizer.plotAllTiles(featuresRDD, 0 to 2, 256, 5, opts)

// Save the tiles to a compact ZIP archive
val outZip = s"$output_dir/output_mvt.zip"
MVTDataVisualizer.saveTilesCompact(tiles, outZip, opts)

// Verify the output ZIP file was created and is not empty
val outPath = new Path(outZip)
val fs = outPath.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val exists = fs.exists(outPath)
val size = if (exists) fs.getFileStatus(outPath).getLen else 0L

assert(exists, "Output ZIP file was not created")
assert(size > 0, "Output ZIP file is empty")

println(s"__CHECK__ saveTilesCompact $size")

selectFiles.code:
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
import org.locationtech.jts.geom.{Envelope, GeometryFactory, PrecisionModel}
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor.RasterFileRDD

val tifPath = new Path(raster_tif)
val fs = tifPath.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val dir = tifPath.getParent.toString

// Ensure the index is built before querying to guarantee pruning behavior
val indexPath = new Path(tifPath.getParent, "_index.csv").toString
RasterFileRDD.buildIndex(sc, dir, indexPath)

// Create a query range that covers the whole world to ensure it overlaps with the raster
val geometryFactory = new GeometryFactory(new PrecisionModel(PrecisionModel.FLOATING_SINGLE), 4326)
val queryRange = geometryFactory.toGeometry(new Envelope(-180, 180, -90, 90))

// Query the files
val matchingFiles = RasterFileRDD.selectFiles(fs, dir, queryRange)

// Verify the result
assert(matchingFiles != null, "Resulting array should not be null")
assert(matchingFiles.length > 0, "Should find at least one matching file")
assert(matchingFiles.exists(_.contains(tifPath.getName)), "The original raster file should be in the selected files")

println(s"__CHECK__ selectFiles ${matchingFiles.length}")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

val opts = new BeastOptions()
val updatedOpts = opts.setBoolean("test.boolean.true", true)
                      .setBoolean("test.boolean.false", false)

// BeastOptions inherits from Hadoop's Configuration, so we can read the values back
val valTrue = updatedOpts.getBoolean("test.boolean.true", false)
val valFalse = updatedOpts.getBoolean("test.boolean.false", true)

assert(valTrue == true, "test.boolean.true was not set correctly")
assert(valFalse == false, "test.boolean.false was not set correctly")

println(s"__CHECK__ setBoolean $valTrue, $valFalse")

slidingWindow.code:
val smoothedRaster = RasterOperationsFocal.slidingWindow(
  rasterRDD, 
  1, 
  (vals: Array[Float], defs: Array[Boolean]) => {
    val validVals = vals.indices.filter(i => defs(i)).map(i => vals(i))
    if (validVals.isEmpty) 0f else validVals.sum / validVals.length
  }
)

val originalCount = rasterRDD.count()
val smoothedCount = smoothedRaster.count()

assert(smoothedCount == originalCount, s"Expected $originalCount tiles after sliding window, but got $smoothedCount")
assert(smoothedCount > 0, "Expected non-empty raster")

println(s"__CHECK__ slidingWindow $smoothedCount")

spatialFile.code:
val spatialRDD = sc.spatialFile(vector_geojson, "geojson")

val count = spatialRDD.count()
val firstFeature = spatialRDD.first()
val geom = firstFeature.getGeometry

assert(count > 0, "Loaded SpatialRDD should contain at least one feature")
assert(geom != null && geom.getGeometryType != null, "First feature must have a valid parsed geometry")
assert(!geom.isEmpty, "Parsed geometry should not be empty")

println(s"__CHECK__ spatialFile count=$count, first_geom_type=${geom.getGeometryType}")

spatialJoinBNLJ.code:
val joinedRDD = SpatialJoin.spatialJoinBNLJ(
  featuresRDD, 
  featuresRDD, 
  ESJPredicate.MBRIntersects
)

val joinCount = joinedRDD.count()
val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()

// In a self-join with MBRIntersects, every feature intersects with its own MBR, 
// so the join count must be at least the number of features in the original RDD.
assert(joinCount >= originalCount, s"Self-join count $joinCount should be >= originalCount $originalCount")
println(s"__CHECK__ spatialJoinBNLJ $joinCount")

spatialJoinPBSM.code:
val predicate = edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.cg.SpatialJoinAlgorithms.ESJPredicate.MBRIntersects
val joinedRDD = edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.operations.SpatialJoin.spatialJoinPBSM(featuresRDD, featuresRDD, predicate)

val joinCount = joinedRDD.count()
val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()

// A self-join with MBRIntersects must yield at least as many pairs as there are original features,
// since every feature's MBR intersects with itself.
assert(joinCount >= originalCount, s"Self-join count $joinCount should be >= original count $originalCount")
println(s"__CHECK__ spatialJoinPBSM $joinCount")

  new Coordinate(170.0, 10.0)
)

tileIDs.code:
import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

val path = new Path(raster_tif)
val fileSystem = path.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val reader = new GeoTiffReader()
reader.initialize(fileSystem, raster_tif, "0", new BeastOptions())

val ids: Iterator[Int] = reader.metadata.tileIDs
val idsList = ids.toList
val numTiles = idsList.size

// The number of tile IDs in the metadata should match the number of tiles loaded in the high-level RasterRDD
val expectedNumTiles = rasterRDD.count().toInt

assert(numTiles == expectedNumTiles, s"Expected $expectedNumTiles tile IDs, but got $numTiles")
assert(numTiles > 0, "Tile IDs iterator should not be empty")
assert(idsList.distinct.size == numTiles, "Tile IDs should be unique")

println(s"__CHECK__ tileIDs $numTiles")

uniform.code:
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.generator._

val numBoxes = 1000L
val randomBoxes = sc.generateSpatialData
  .config(UniformDistribution.GeometryType, "box")
  .config(UniformDistribution.MaxSize, "0.01,0.01")
  .uniform(numBoxes)

val count = randomBoxes.count()
assert(count == numBoxes, s"Expected $numBoxes boxes, but got $count")

println(s"__CHECK__ uniform $count")

uniformHistogramCount.code:
val histogramSize = Array(10, 10)
val hist = featuresRDD.uniformHistogramCount(histogramSize, prefixSum = true)

val totalFeatures = featuresRDD.count()
// Query the entire grid range to get the total count of feature-cell intersections
val histTotal = hist.getValue(Array(0, 0), histogramSize)

// Since features can span multiple cells, the sum of all cells must be at least the total number of features
assert(histTotal >= totalFeatures, s"Histogram total count ($histTotal) should be >= total features ($totalFeatures)")

println(s"__CHECK__ uniformHistogramCount $histTotal")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._

val numBins = sizeHistogram.getNumBins
assert(numBins == 100, s"Expected 100 bins for a 10x10 histogram, but got $numBins")

println(s"__CHECK__ uniformHistogramSize $numBins")

writeSpatialFile.code:
val outPath = s"$output_dir/test_writeSpatialFile.geojson"
val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()

// Write the features to a GeoJSON file using the extension method
featuresRDD.coalesce(1).writeSpatialFile(outPath, "geojson")

// Read the written file back to verify its contents
val readBack = sc.spatialFile(outPath, "geojson")
val count = readBack.count()

assert(count == originalCount, s"Expected $originalCount features to be written and read back, but got $count")
println(s"__CHECK__ writeSpatialFile $count")

x1.code:
val tile = rasterRDD.first()
val x1 = tile.x1
val x2 = tile.x2

// The starting column index (x1) must be less than or equal to the ending column index (x2)
assert(x1 <= x2, s"Tile x1 ($x1) must be less than or equal to x2 ($x2)")

// Verify that iterating using x1 and x2 covers the expected number of columns
val expectedColumns = x2 - x1 + 1
assert(expectedColumns > 0, s"Tile must have a positive number of columns, derived $expectedColumns from x1=$x1 and x2=$x2")

println(s"__CHECK__ x1 $x1")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor.{ZonalStatistics, Statistics}

val opts = new BeastOptions()

// Call ZonalStatistics.zonalStats2 statically
val zsResults = ZonalStatistics.zonalStats2(
  featuresRDD, 
  rasterRDD, 
  classOf[Statistics], 
  opts
)

// Extracting statistics requires casting the Collector
val processedStats = zsResults.map { case (feature, collector) =>
  val stats = collector.asInstanceOf[Statistics]
  val count = stats.count(0)
  val sum = stats.sum(0)
  (count, sum)
}

val resultsArray = processedStats.collect()
val numFeatures = featuresRDD.count()

// The number of zones with statistics should not exceed the total number of input features
assert(resultsArray.length <= numFeatures, s"Result size ${resultsArray.length} exceeds input features $numFeatures")

val totalCount = resultsArray.map(_._1).sum
val totalSum = resultsArray.map(_._2).sum

println(s"__CHECK__ zonalStats2 zones_processed=${resultsArray.length} total_
